Variables.Name: bDebugMode
Variables.Description:
If false, UnrealLightmass.exe is launched automatically (default)
	 	If true, it must be launched manually (e.g. through a debugger) with the -debug command line parameter.

Variables.Name: bStatsEnabled
Variables.Description: If true, all participating Lightmass agents will report back detailed stats to the log.

Variables.Name: bGatherBSPSurfacesAcrossComponents
Variables.Description: If true, BSP surfaces split across model components are joined into 1 mapping

Variables.Name: CoplanarTolerance
Variables.Description: The tolerance level used when gathering BSP surfaces.

Variables.Name: bUseImmediateImport
Variables.Description:
If true, Lightmass will import mappings immediately as they complete.
	 	It will not process them, however.

Variables.Name: bImmediateProcessMappings
Variables.Description:
If true, Lightmass will process appropriate mappings as they are imported.
	 	NOTE: Requires ImmediateMode be enabled to actually work.

Variables.Name: bDebugMaterials
Variables.Description:
If true, Lightmass will write out BMPs for each generated material property
	 	sample to <GAME>\ScreenShots\Materials.

Variables.Name: bSortMappings
Variables.Description: If true, Lightmass will sort mappings by texel cost.

Variables.Name: bDumpBinaryFiles
Variables.Description: If true, the generate coefficients will be dumped to binary files.

Variables.Name: bPadMappings
Variables.Description: If true, Lightmass will pad the calculated mappings to reduceeliminate seams.

Variables.Name: bDebugPaddings
Variables.Description:
If true, will fill padding of mappings with a color rather than the sampled edges.
	 	Means nothing if bPadMappings is not enabled...

Variables.Name: bOnlyCalcDebugTexelMappings
Variables.Description:
If true, only the mapping containing a debug texel will be calculated, all others
	  will be set to white

Variables.Name: ExecutionTimeDivisor
Variables.Description: The amount of time that will be count as full red when bColorByExecutionTime is enabled

Variables.Name: bUseRandomColors
Variables.Description: If true, color lightmaps a random color

Variables.Name: bColorBordersGreen
Variables.Description: If true, a green border will be placed around the edges of mappings

Variables.Name: bColorByExecutionTime
Variables.Description:
If true, Lightmass will overwrite lightmap data with a shade of red relating to
	  how long it took to calculate the mapping (Red = Time  ExecutionTimeDivisor)
